steps.title: Compute first-year earnings for each account
steps.content:
Account A earns 6% annual interest on 500 dollars:
$$0.06 \times 500 = 30$$
Account B earns a flat 25 dollars per year. So in year 1, Account A earns 30 dollars and Account B earns 25 dollars.

steps.title: Track earnings in later years
steps.content:
Account A compounds annually, so its balance grows each year and so do its earnings:
Year 2 balance = 530 dollars, earnings = $$0.06 \times 530 = 31.80$$
Year 3 balance = 561.80 dollars, earnings = $$0.06 \times 561.80 \approx 33.71$$
Account B keeps earning a fixed 25 dollars every year. Since A starts above 25 dollars and only grows, A is always greater than B.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — Account A always earns more money per year than Account B. A starts at \$30 and grows; B stays at \$25. True.
  • Choice B — Account A always earns less money per year than Account B. False — even in year 1, A earns \$30 vs. B's \$25.
  • Choice C — Account A earns more money per year than Account B at first but eventually earns less money per year. A's earnings only grow, so it never drops below \$25. False.
  • Choice D — Account A earns less money per year than Account B at first but eventually earns more money per year. A is already higher in year 1. False.
That is the correct answer.
